idea: Avoid tokio runtime startup for synchronous --list/--install/--config/--update subcommands by replacing #[tokio::main] with a plain main and only building the runtime for async modes.

Defer tracing_subscriber::fmt().init() until after the synchronous fast-path subcommands have returned — --list, --install, --config, --update don't emit any tracing events, so the global subscriber, env filter parsing, and stderr writer registration are pure overhead.
Add panic="abort" to [profile.release] — eliminates unwind tables, landing pads, and personality routines, shrinking the binary and reducing dynamic loader/relocation work.

Switch [profile.release] from lto='thin' to lto='fat' (full cross-crate LTO). Slower compile, but enables more aggressive dead-code elimination across crate boundaries — should shrink the on-disk binary and reduce dynamic linker / page-fault work at startup.
